column1: # Owner: Jane. Machine-readable. John: parse this, don't hardcode weapon numbers.
# Tab-separated. '#' comments and blank lines ignored.
# One row per (weapon, level). Values are ABSOLUTE, not deltas — no formula for
# you to reimplement, and I can hand-tune any single cell.
# ALL DISTANCES ARE WORLD UNITS. design.md §5: 1 cell = 1 wu wide x 2 wu tall.
# A band 6 wu tall renders as 3 rows. A ring of radius r draws as an ellipse
# bolt     hit radius        hit radius      wu/s, homing
# arc      burst radius      burst radius    wu/s, lobbed
# orbit    orbit radius      hit radius      degrees/s
# cd=0 means "always on" (orbit). pierce 99 = effectively infinite.
# dur = lifetime of the effect in seconds (bolts, embers, columns).
# count = simultaneous instances (bolts fired, orbiting motes, columns).

column16: bands BOTH sides from lv1 (front band wider). no longer the starting weapon.
adds a vertical band — a cross
lobs up, falls, shatters; ax = burst radius
ax = orbit radius, ay = hit radius, pspeed = deg/s
falling column in a random zone near you
burning embers behind you; dur = ember lifetime
